title: Fire-kraken
content:
{{Top|ca|leg|notoc=1}} {{Otheruses|fire|more=kraken (disambiguation)}} {{Species |image= |name=Fire-kraken |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Non-sentient]]{{CSWECite|I|274|fire-kraken}} |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=[[Snake/Legends|Snake]]-like tendrils''[[The Cestus Deception]]'' |lifespan= |origin=[[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Goodness gracious! So dangerous! The fire-kraken…''"
"''Are no longer an issue.|[[Doolb Snoil]] and Kit Fisto discuss the fire-kraken|}} The '''fire-kraken''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Ocean/Legends|marine]] predator that dwelt in the deepest oceanic trenches of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] within the [[Y'Toub system/Legends|Y'Toub system]] of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} Their most distinguishing feature was the [[Snake/Legends|snake]]-like tendrils they possessed, which they used to locate and capture prey. The species' presence in the trenches made [[Diver|trench-diving]] on Nal Hutta particularly dangerous; however in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the [[Nautolan/Legends|Nautolan]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Kit Fisto]] claimed that they were no longer a problem, having previously spent a [[Standard week/Legends|week]] trench diving on Hutta himself. ==Behind the scenes== Fire-kraken were first mentioned in the [[2004]] novel ''[[The Cestus Deception]]'', which was written by [[Steven Barnes]], and also received an entry in the [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]]. The novel used the plural to refer to the fire-kraken, suggesting there were more than one although the entry in the encyclopedia uses the singular suggesting that there was only one fire-kraken. This article assumes the former is correct and that the encyclopedia referred to a specific member of an entire species. ==Appearances== *''[[The Cestus Deception]]'' {{1stm}} *[[The Cestus Deception (abridged audiobook)|''The Cestus Deception'' abridged audiobook]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Aquatic creatures]] [[Category:Creatures of Nal Hutta]] [[Category:Creatures of unspecified biology]] [[Category:Predatory creatures]] [[Category:Species of undefined sentience]]
